summary: Iteration 3 backend testing: deck system, custom vocabulary, library, writing-recognition (GPT-4o-mini vision), deck-gated /flashcards/new, lesson in_deck annotation, review.mode field, and legacy deck migration. 47/47 pytest pass (100%): 24 carry-over (test_backend.py with 3 updated to seed deck first, since /flashcards/new is now deck-gated by spec), 6 whisper, 17 new (test_deck_and_writing.py).
action_items: (carryover from iter1) Profile logout uses Alert.alert -> no-op on Expo Web; needs Platform-aware confirm.
OPTIONAL: server.py is now ~960 lines (>700 guideline). Split into routers/{auth,lessons,flashcards,deck,vocabulary,speaking,writing,progress}.py.
OPTIONAL: /api/writing/recognize and /api/speaking/transcribe leak raw exception messages in the 502 detail string; consider sanitizing.

OPTIONAL: GET /api/vocabulary/library currently does `query[lesson_number] = N` AFTER `query[lesson_number] = {"$ne": None}` when both source=npcr and lesson_number are provided — the second assignment overwrites the first (works correctly because lesson_number=N already implies non-null, but is fragile).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: 965 lines — past the 700-line guideline. High priority to split into APIRouter modules.

create_custom_vocab: doc.pop('_id', None) is called AFTER motor's insert_one mutates the doc in place with _id. Good — but the response uses the same dict that already has _id stripped at return time. Verified working in tests (dict returns clean JSON).
/api/deck enrichment loops per entry (N+1 query: deck → vocabulary → flashcard). Acceptable for small decks; switch to aggregation $lookup if a power user adds 500+ cards.
/api/vocabulary/library: when source is None, wraps query in $and with {$or:[lesson_id ne null, created_by=user]}. Verified that user A does NOT see user B's custom vocab.
Legacy migrate_legacy_decks runs on every startup with an aggregation across all flashcards. For O(thousands of users) consider gating behind a feature flag or one-time migration.

/api/writing/recognize: image_base64 length>100 check accepts garbage that's longer than 100 chars but not a valid image — that's fine because GPT-4o-mini call then returns 502; no security issue, but a magic-bytes sniff would surface 400 faster.
Pydantic models still use .dict() (deprecated in v2). Minor warning only.
Review history `mode` defaults to 'reading' even when not provided — good. No validation that mode is one of {reading,writing,speaking}; user could send arbitrary string. Recommend an Enum/Literal.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_backend.py (3 TestFlashcardsSRS tests updated to seed deck before calling /flashcards/new, matching new deck-gated contract)
seed_data_creation: Each pytest run creates ~3 TEST_<uuid>@example.com users (auth_token, fresh_user from conftest, plus per-module fresh_user in test_deck_and_writing). Also creates one custom vocab '测试词' (cleaned up by test_delete_custom_only_by_owner). test@test.com flashcards/deck preserved; legacy migration on backend startup correctly back-filled 8 user_deck entries for test@test.com from existing flashcards (verified in backend logs and via TestLegacyMigration.test_test_user_deck_matches_flashcards).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run: `python -m pytest backend/tests/ -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml`. EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL drives all tests. The writing-recognize endpoint accepts both 200 and 502 on the live OpenAI call (200 if GPT-4o-mini responds for a 1x1 PNG, 502 if it errors) — only the auth gating, validation (400 for <100-char base64), and response shape are strictly asserted. flashcards/new is now DECK-GATED — any test that previously assumed NPCR vocab auto-appears must first POST /api/deck/add. Frontend handwriting canvas was explicitly out of scope this iteration per E1's note.
rca_of_issue: No bugs found. The 3 original TestFlashcardsSRS failures were stale fixtures — fixed by inserting `_seed_deck(api, fresh_user, 5)` helper before each /flashcards/new call, which matches the new spec where users must explicitly add vocab to their deck before SRS picks it up.
